title: Clinging to the last patches
author: OpenDiscover
blurb: Sixty percent of the habitat is gone, yet the species holds on — settling at 20% patch occupancy in Levins' metapopulation model. The catch is the extinction threshold: colonization c=0.5 and extinction e=0.1 put the cliff at 80% destroyed (1 − e/c). Bulldoze past it and the whole network collapses, sometimes long after the last patch is cleared. That lag is the 'extinction debt' — species that are already doomed but not yet gone (Tilman et al., 1994).
